sanskrit:
श्रीभगवानुवाच।
मय्यासक्तमनाः पार्थ योगं युञ्जन्मदाश्रयः।
असंशयं समग्रं मां यथा ज्ञास्यसि तच्छृणु।।
transliteration:
śrī-bhagavān uvāca
mayy āsakta-manāḥ pārtha yogaṁ yuñjan mad-āśrayaḥ
asaṁśayaṁ samagraṁ māṁ yathā jñāsyasi tac chṛṇu
literal_meaning: The Blessed Lord said: 'Hear how, O Pārtha, with mind attached to Me, practicing yoga taking refuge in Me, without doubt and completely, you shall know Me.'
contextual_meaning: Krishna shifts from self-realization techniques to relational knowing: full knowledge of the Absolute emerges when attention is emotionally bonded to Him and all other ways of knowing are surrendered.
psychological_state: Transition from anxious self-reliance to secure attachment; mind moves from problem-solving mode to receptive listening.
trigger_scenario: You’ve tried every self-help method yet still feel something’s missing; a trusted mentor says, ‘Stop fixing—just listen to me.’
anchor_text: Know Me completely by loving Me with your whole mind.
